ops.body:
Closed in abwc 0.12.1 (cc-skills origin/main 2f3e2ea). C1: create_session writes the new id to a mktemp marker file in-subshell before the POST returns, and the EXIT/signal trap releases the marker's id too — closing the create→read→arm leak window a shell variable couldn't. C2: `ab close` is an idempotent bounded reap-all loop (releases every tagged session, resumes after an interrupt; a transient GET failure retries rather than concluding empty). C3: SLUG now appends an 8-hex shasum-256 suffix of the full raw requestor TAG, so the non-injective sanitizer (a/b vs a?b → ab-a-b) can no longer collide distinct requestors onto one session; deterministic in TAG so find_session still matches.

The prior double-release-on-second-signal edge was already closed in 0.11.0. Residual (documented, not a criterion): the `cookiesync requestor` PID fallback outside a Claude session defeats persistence; the sub-printf marker window and the clear-before-exec window are irreducible in bash (SESSION_TIMEOUT is the backstop).
